problems.symptom: Wire Feed Motor Runs but Wire Does Not Feed Properly

causes_and_solutions.solution: Increase wire feed pressure. Follow tension test: feed wire against wood block at 2-3 inches, wire should bend.
Flip roller to correct size. V-Groove for solid core, Knurled for flux-cored.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Damaged MIG Gun, cable, or liner assembly
causes_and_solutions.solution: Have qualified technician inspect and replace as necessary.
Loosen Feed Tensioner so it only applies enough pressure to prevent spinning after trigger release.

problems.symptom: Wire Creates a Bird's Nest During Operation

causes_and_solutions.solution: Adjust wire feed pressure properly. Tension should be 3-5 for solid, 2-3 for flux-cored.
Replace with proper tip for wire used.

causes_and_solutions.cause: MIG Gun Cable Connector not fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism
causes_and_solutions.solution: Insert Gun Cable Connector fully — no O-Rings should be exposed.
Have qualified technician inspect and repair/replace.
Straighten Gun cable.
Check gun liner for obstruction, replace if necessary.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Gun liner too small for wire
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check that gun liner is correct size for wire.
Check wire for cross winding or tangled spool.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Wire not in Feed Roller groove
causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure correct groove for wire diameter is being used.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Feed Roller not making enough contact / crushing flux-cored wire
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check Feed Tensioner and ensure proper setting (2-3 for flux-cored).
See wire feed troubleshooting above.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Incorrect Contact Tip or liner size / excessive wear
causes_and_solutions.solution: Replace with proper tip or liner size.
Adjust wire feed speed to achieve more stable arc.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Loose MIG Gun cable or ground cable
causes_and_solutions.solution: Check all connections are tight.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Damaged MIG Gun or loose internal connection
causes_and_solutions.solution: Have technician inspect and repair.
DCEP for MIG, DCEN for Flux-Cored self-shielded.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Gas coverage insufficient or too high (MIG only)
causes_and_solutions.solution: Set gas flow to 20-30 SCFH. Ensure Cable Connector is fully inserted.
Check ground clamp connection. Ensure MIG Gun is properly secured.
Check line voltage, have electrician remedy if insufficient.
Do NOT use extension cord. Use only supplied power cords.
Switch current to proper setting for metal thickness.

problems.symptom: Wire Feeds but Arc Does Not Ignite

causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure workpiece is properly contacted by Ground Clamp, cleaned near clamp and weld location.
Verify Contact Tip matches wire size.
Check hole is not deformed or enlarged, replace if needed.
Clean Contact Tip properly.

problems.symptom: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On

causes_and_solutions.solution: Wait with Power Switch ON for cooling. Warning screen shows in LCD. Reduce welding duration.
Verify circuit supplies required specs. Check for low/high voltage warning on LCD.
Ensure gun connection is properly seated. Technician must check Trigger.
Check input voltage is within range. Press Reset Button on back of machine.
Ensure correct process has been selected on LCD.

problems.symptom: LCD Display Does Not Light When Switched On

causes_and_solutions.solution: Verify voltage at outlet and connection.
Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices.
Make sure installed plug is correct rating per Specifications.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Circuit breaker tripped due to high input amperage
causes_and_solutions.solution: Press Reset Button on back of machine.

causes_and_solutions.cause: Input Power Cord not seated properly
causes_and_solutions.solution: Ensure twist lock input Power Cord is fully secured.
Check gas bottle and replenish.
Check gas regulator for proper flow (20-30 SCFH).
Clean workpiece down to bare metal.
Check CTWD — maintain 1/2" or less.
DCEP for MIG, DCEN for Flux-Cored.
Ensure wire is clean, free of rust and residues.

problems.symptom: Welder Does Not Function When Switched On

causes_and_solutions.solution: Reduce duration/frequency of welding. Follow duty cycle guidelines.
Qualified technician must check and secure/replace Trigger.
Attach Ground Clamp to workpiece.
Connect shielding gas to Welder.

problems.symptom: LCD Display Does Not Light When Switched On

causes_and_solutions.solution: Verify voltage at outlet and connection.
Check circuit breaker/GFCI devices. Verify circuit supplies required input amperage.
Check line voltage, have electrician remedy.
Do NOT use extension cord. Use only supplied power cords.
Check all connections are tight.
Have technician inspect and repair.
Match setting to recommended chart value.
Replace shielding gas cylinder.

too_cold.appearance: Weld does not penetrate the joint fully, just on the surface

proper.appearance: Weld is visible underneath and bulges slightly on top

too_hot.appearance: Weld droops on top and underneath, or falls through entirely making a hole

bead_problems.problem: Voltage Too Low / Wire Feed Too Slow
bead_problems.appearance: Tall, narrow, ropey bead with poor fusion
bead_problems.fix: Increase Output Voltage OR Increase Wire Feed Speed

bead_problems.problem: Voltage Too High / Wire Feed Too Fast
bead_problems.appearance: Wide, flat bead with excessive spatter
bead_problems.fix: Decrease Output Voltage OR Decrease Wire Feed Speed

bead_problems.appearance: Thin, stringy bead with poor penetration
Wide, built-up bead with excess material
Irregular bead with excessive spatter and poor fusion
bead_problems.fix: Check polarity (DCEP for MIG, DCEN for Flux-Cored) AND maintain less than 1/2" CTWD

bead_problems.appearance: Small cavities or holes in the bead
Grainy and large spatter around weld (fine spatter is normal)
Base material melts away leaving a hole
Gaps between weld and workpiece or previous bead

too_cold.appearance: Weld does not contact the joint fully, just on the surface

proper.appearance: Weld is visible underneath and bulges slightly on top

too_hot.appearance: Weld droops on top and underneath or falls through entirely, making a hole

bead_problems.appearance: Small cavities or holes in bead

findings.result: Both produced good welds. Synergetic presets worked well.

findings.extra_step: Change roller from knurled to V-groove, add 100% CO2 shielding
findings.result: Synergetic settings spot on. 'Two for two.'

findings.arc_start: Lift-style TIG available. Foot pedal provides amperage control.
findings.suggestion: Add high-frequency start for quicker ignition
findings.result: Functional but lift-start is slower than HF start

findings.notes: Machine prompts for 60xx or 70xx series. 6010 compatibility unclear.
findings.result: Good weld on plate and fillet on 1/8" coupons

video_observations.overall_assessment: Synergetic presets work well for all processes. Machine sits mid-range in feature set. Versatile and functional across all four processes.
